item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read this on mobile today. Really enjoyed it, read the whole thing w/o skimming, and even went back and forth to pick up some things I missed, which is proof I was invested.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to toss out my guesses for Document 4066-κ-5. For the most part, I had the same answers as the above, except I guessed Wormwood/4008 for the butterfly line.

spoilers
– hide block

First of all, I guessed that the daemon as a whole was a reference for Bush v Gore, but evidently that was incorrect.

saw a halo of flames around the Empress' head, from which golden eyes stared in all directions; and of course he swore that it was a member of the lower orders of angels, sent to advise the empress of the Lord's will

My only guess for this one is SCP-4005, but I'm not sure about it.

believed that the Empress was constantly circled at her waist by a five flying discs, each of a different precious stone, that passed through all solid matter as they orbited her

I can't justify it, but I feel as though this is SCP-4017 in my gut.

curls of smoke emerged from the Empress' ears and nose, and coiled upwards in intricate spirals

I took this to be the demon from SCP-4300.

swirling black lines crawling, snake-like, across the Empress' face; these lines would sometimes leave her skin, travel in the air for a short distance, and alight on her once more

I thought this was EoI-4400-Caceres.

some of black iron and some of red brass, bloodlessly penetrating her head and chest; they sank in and raised back up, sometimes in regular waves and sometimes without any pattern he could see

I have no idea about this one.

claimed it appeared as a squid, or an octopus, whose tentacles curled around her head; only she could hear its voice, which was that of an old woman, but eloquent and refined, as if it had been trained as an orator.

I think this is a reference to SCP-4540, but it could also be Metaphysician's deleted entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok so I got none of the references.
I half thought of the constructs as space marines but that was me thinking "No, that couldn't possible be true". That said, I would have personally not picked this for the 4000 entry (due to others) but this is definitly deserving of being an SCP. Written lovely like a true historial document, citing both sources, dates and writers. Dumb as they may seem, I love it all together.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I experienced a slight snag with Document 4066-α-17. Based on the tone, I assumed Julian was being sarcastic. It wasn't until I reached the end without encountering a punchline that I realized he had been serious.

I'd say I've been reading too much Shakespere, but I've never sat down and actually read any Shakespere. Purely through cultural osmosis, I've somehow been rendered tone-deaf to bible-talk. I'm not sure if there's anything the author can do about this or not, but I'm mentioning it so we can take the temperature of the rest of the audience.

Was my interpretation a fluke? Or is "Oh, lo, great and wise spirit, please bestow upon me your impossible wisdom!" a subverted meme in contemporary culture?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remains of four powered-armor exoskeletons, heavily battle-damaged and with the internal power sources removed. Instances are engraved with passages from Christian scriptures, and each has a panel on its chest depicting one of the four winged creatures named in the book of Revelation and associated with the four Christian evangelists: a lion, an ox, an angel and an eagle.

…so basically Space Marines with Christ instead of God-Emperor?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes! And for the reference you weren't sure about, here is your answer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since you challenged us to decipher the parasite's references, and because that's an exercise that will get harder once series 5 opens up properly, I present my guesses within a spoiler, in order:

spoilers
spoilers
  • A Dream Come True
  • Socratic Containment Procedures
  • Bush v. Gore (I think. Not sure what the reference to a butterfly is about)
  • Kedesh-Nanaya
  • Future Imperfect
  • Those Who Don't Learn From History
  • Taboo
spoilers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tiberius III Apsimar was a Scientologist. Further information is restricted to personnel assigned to Project FROZEN SOUL and the Temporal Anomalies Department.

Damn, this got a solid half minute or so of laughter from me.

Overall there is not much for me to say about this article. You basically took a bunch of small ideas and molded them into an amalgamation of sci-fi, humor, and History Channel tv show scripts. I think the episodic approach here really works, as it allows you to make a rapid fire of different ways the Byzantine empire was affected by aliens and the variety present is quite exquisite.

+1. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By the time I got to the possessed/parasitized queen, I was feeling it.

Shortly after, with the abducted emperor's perfectly calm and reasonable relation of being abducted and implanted with a tracking device, I was hooked. I love it.

Only thing: with regards to that parasite, wouldn't it have been more of a symbiote?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved the heck out of this. I imagine that there's a fair number of historical in-jokes that flew over my head, but it was still really entertaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this, love the length, the details, the cross linking, everything. This is an excellent and exemplary scp article. Didn’t horrify me but after a few thousand articles I kind of enjoy the not so horrific ones more.

+1 and +10 if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, mnestics. These are drugs that allow things to be remembered such as antimemes, think the opposite of amnestics. Antimemes are ideas or things that are hard to pass on, remember, or even perceive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know nearly enough about the Byzantine Empire to get the references, so this is just boring for me.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Δεν λέω ότι ήταν αλλοδαποί, αλλά ήταν εξωγήινοι. One suggestion, switch out the Emperor who's listed as being a Scientologist to being a Fifthist, I think it'd fit better within the Foundation conceptual space.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
small suggestions
– hide block

chewing mouthparts similar to grasshoppers

"Chelicerae" may be a more scientific way of wording that phrase.

mild antimemetic effect, causing observers to perceive them as depictions of human beings; as a result, the anomalous nature of SCP-4000-β was not discovered until the first use of mnestic drugs by the Foundation in the 1980s.

Do amnestics technically counter memetic effects? I don't know what the consensus is, thought memetics were counteracted with anti-memetics, but I don't know, amnestics may be a subset of anti-memetics for all I know.

This is a lot, lot to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is as daring as it is dry, which could just be because I spent so much time reading about Byzantium in the quest of writing my own entry. I'll probably upvote after the contest.

the anomalous nature of SCP-4000-β was not discovered until the first use of mnestic drugs by the Foundation in the 1980s.

That must have been a hell of a shock! I now want a tale about the development of mnestics, and how long it took the Foundation to realize that "those wacky drugs" weren't causing people to hallucinate things that weren't there, but see the truth no one else could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is stupid as hell.

+1

I think this would be improved with some images to break up the text, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
automatic upvote because of Byzantium.

thanks for giving attention to these beautiful bastards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was my 4000-con entry; it came in 48th. The seed of the idea came from Randomini's Cool War 2; I have no idea how this fits into the Cool War 2 canon, but I'm sure someone will make it work. Irene's Daimon is inspired by Communism will win's 2000-con magnum opus, "Cliche, Right?"; can you figure out all the references?

Critiqued by Roget and UraniumEmpire.
